name: Braised Breast of Duck/Chicken with Peaches
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Light vegetable oil (such as safflower)
4 to 6 half breasts of duck or chicken, skinned and boned
1 tablespoon Guava or red currant jelly
1/2 cup Hollandaise sauce (or 1/3 cup heavy cream, whipped)
4 to 6 fresh peaches, peeled and quartered
recipeInstructions:
1. In a deep, heavy pot, heat the oil.
2. Add 2 tablespoons butter and let it melt and foam.
3. Add mushrooms and stir over high heat for 2 minutes. Remove mushrooms with a slotted spoon and set aside.
4. Brown the livers; remove and set aside.
5. Lightly brown the duck or chicken breasts on both sides, then remove from the pan.
6. Reduce the heat to low, add another tablespoon of butter and melt it.
7. Add garlic and orange rind. Stir over low heat for 2 minutes.
8. Add tomato paste and meat flavoring, then blend.
9. Add stock, orange juice, and honey. Stir over moderate heat until the mixture boils.
10. Add peach puree and jelly, then bring to a boil.
11. Place the breasts in the pot and coat with sauce. Cook over low heat for about 20 minutes or until done.
12. Arrange the breasts on a heatproof serving platter.
13. Whisk the Hollandaise sauce (or whipped cream) into the sauce.
14. Add mushrooms and peaches.
15. Spoon the sauce mixture over the breasts and brown the top lightly under the broiler.
16. Slice the livers neatly and arrange as garnish on top of the dish.
17. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
